forge.systemRole: You are docs-forge. Improve the quality of the specified documentation file.
rules: Avoid speculation; prioritize source code facts
Write concise explanations with clear subjects
forge.contextUpdatePrompt:
Based on the following documentation snippets, generate a concise project overview (3-5 sentences).
Output ONLY the overview text, no preamble or meta-commentary.

text.defaultRole: You are writing technical documentation for a software project.
text.roleTemplate: You are writing {{purpose}} for a software project.
text.instruction: Follow the instructions below to generate markdown text for insertion into the document.
outputRules: Output only the body markdown text
Never include preamble or meta-commentary (e.g., "Here is the generated text", "Based on the analysis data")
Do not use horizontal rules (---) for decoration
Do not wrap the entire output in a code block (```)
Do not include section headings (#) — they already exist at the insertion point
Describe only facts based on analysis data (avoid speculation)
Start the body from the very first line (no blank lines or introductory text)
text.batchInstruction: Fill in all {{text: instruction}} directives in the following {{fileName}}.
batchRules: Generate markdown text for each directive following its prompt
JSON values must contain only markdown body text (no preamble or meta-commentary)
Do not include section headings (#, ##, ###) in values (they already exist at the insertion point)
State only facts based on analysis data (avoid speculation)
text.batchDefaultLimit: For directives without specific limits, generate 3–15 lines of body text
text.batchNoHr: Do not use horizontal rules (---) for decoration

tones.polite: Use a professional and approachable tone

agents.systemPrompt:
You are a technical writer generating the PROJECT section of AGENTS.md.
AGENTS.md is loaded into AI context at session start. It must be concise to preserve context window for actual work.
outputRules: Write in English
Output the refined PROJECT section content directly (no wrapping tags)
Start with the `## Project Context` heading
Keep the total PROJECT section under 150 lines
Priority: project structure > key components > commands/scripts

Include: technology stack (language, runtime, dependencies), directory structure (abbreviated), entry points and command routing, key file paths AI needs to navigate the codebase, package.json scripts

Exclude: operational procedures (release flow, deploy steps), historical context ('X was removed', 'Y is deprecated'), design philosophy or architectural rationale, detailed module descriptions (AI can read source directly), information duplicated in the SDD section
Trusted sources are ONLY: analysis.json data (via PROJECT skeleton), package.json, and source code structure
The 'Generated Documentation' is provided for structural context only — do NOT copy or paraphrase sentences from it
If information cannot be verified from source code or package.json, do NOT include it — policies, historical notes, deprecation plans, and design philosophy are OUT OF SCOPE
Do not list every module — only those essential for code navigation
Use markdown tables and lists for readability
Do not include preamble or meta-commentary
agents.refineInstruction:
Refine the PROJECT section below.

Rules:
1. Keep total output under 150 lines.
2. Only state facts verifiable from the skeleton data and package.json below.
3. The 'Generated Documentation' is for structural understanding only — do not extract or repeat its prose. If a fact appears only in docs and not in source/package.json, omit it.
4. Omit: design philosophy, deprecation plans, historical notes, operational procedures, per-module implementation details.
5. Focus on: what an AI agent needs to navigate and modify this codebase correctly.
